close_reason: Replaced elapsed() comparison with checked_add: None means never-expires. Added Duration::MAX test.
description:
src/lib.rs:77: 'if entry.fetched_at.elapsed() < self.ttl'.

elapsed() returns Duration::ZERO if 'now' is somehow before fetched_at (Tokio's Instant is monotonic so this is rare). The comparison itself does not overflow. However, lib.rs:69 uses 'self.ttl == Duration::ZERO' as the disable-cache sentinel. A user who actually wants 'cache forever' might pass Duration::MAX, which works but is an unusual sentinel choice.

Real concern: there is no upper-bound documentation. The cache will hold a value indefinitely until process exit if TTL is large. Recommend:
1. Document the upper-bound semantics in the new() docstring: 'A very large TTL (e.g. Duration::from_secs(86400)) effectively pins the value in memory. There is no maximum; users should choose a TTL that matches their secret rotation cadence.'
2. Optionally accept Option<Duration> in a future API: None = no cache, Some(Duration::MAX) = forever, Some(d) = TTL d. This is a breaking change so leave for v0.4 if/when there's another reason to break.

This is the lowest-priority idiom note in the set; only file-and-leave unless other API churn happens. Probably a docs-only fix.
title: Instant::elapsed() comparison against TTL=Duration::MAX or near-MAX overflows panics
